items.title: Arsenal star Piero Hincapie sent off for covering mouth while confronting opponent at World Cup as he breaks new rule
items.summary: ARSENAL defender Piero Hincapie was SENT OFF as Ecuador crashed out of the World Cup. The South Americans suffered a 2-0 defeat to tournament co-hosts Mexico in their last-32 clash. And the showdown ended on a sour note for Hincapie, 24, as he was given his marching orders for breaking a new Fifa ru

items.title: Chloe Kelly frolics in the ocean in tiny blue bikini as she soaks up the sun in Turks and Caicos
items.summary: CHLOE KELLY has been enjoying some downtime in the Caribbean ahead of returning to Arsenal for pre-season. The attacking midfielder, 28, was awarded an MBE in the King's Birthday Honours list last month. And Kelly has been soaking up the sun in Turks and Caicos before jetting back to London to prepa

items.title: Will Sooryavanshi play for India vs England in summer T20 series?
items.summary: Teenage cricket sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues to leave fans around the world astounded by his incredible feats, and after India left him out of their series against Ireland and promptly lost, the focus now is on whether he will get his shot against England.

items.title: I'd have done the same to get him sent off - 20 years since Rooney's red card
items.summary: Former England striker Wayne Rooney talks about his red card in the 2006 World Cup quarter-final against Portugal, and the reaction of his then teammate Cristiano Ronaldo.

items.title: Mexico storm into World Cup last 16 to book potential England clash
items.summary: A trip to the Azteca Stadium awaits England if they beat DR Congo on Wednesday after Mexico strolled past Ecuador 2-0 to reach the last 16 of the World Cup.

items.title: Mexico rampant at fortress Azteca - and England could be next
items.summary: Mexico have won all four games and not conceded a goal at this World Cup. Their last-16 opponents must also contend with playing at the Azteca Stadium.

items.title: Mexico set up potential England last-16 tie after win over 10-man Ecuador
items.summary: Mexico cruise into the last 16 of the World Cup with a comfortable victory over Ecuador in a weather-delayed contest at the Azteca Stadium.

items.title: Hincapie becomes second player sent off for covering mouth
items.summary: Ecuador defender Piero Hincapie became the second player at the World Cup to be dismissed for covering his mouth while confronting an opponent.

items.title: 'The Azteca erupts!' Quinones fires Mexico into lead
items.summary: Julian Quinones times his run to perfection before rifling home a fierce shot to give Mexico the lead against Ecuador in their round of 32 World Cup match.

items.title: 'I remember like it was yesterday' - Euro 2016 hero Robson-Kanu
items.summary: Hal Robson-Kanu reflects on scoring his iconic goal for Wales against Belgium at Euro 2016, 10 years to the day from the quarter-final in Lille.

items.title: Mexico end World Cup knockout drought with last-32 win over Ecuador in Azteca cauldron
items.summary: They were held up by an electric storm but, after it had cleared, Mexico simply kept the lightning bolts coming. El Tri have broken a hex that had gnawed at the nation’s football psyche for years, winning a World Cup knockout game for the first time since 1986, and the head turner will be the manner

items.title: Mexico 2 Ecuador 0: England sent scary World Cup warning as Mexicans run riot at the Azteca – and now await Three Lions
items.summary: MEXICO stormed into the last-16 of the World Cup - and sent a warning to potential opponents England in doing so. The co-hosts blew Ecuador away at a raucous Azteca Stadium after thunder and lightning delayed their clash by an hour. Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez scored first-half goals to put Mex

items.title: Haaland, 'the greatest goalscorer,' saves Norway
items.summary: Norway coach Stale Solbakken described Erling Haaland as the "greatest goal scorer in the world and I wouldn't swap him for anybody."

items.title: Deschamps 'touched' by Mbappe's goal tribute
items.summary: France coach Didier Deschamps said he was deeply moved by Kylian Mbappé's embrace after the captain dedicated his opening goal to him during Les Bleus' 3-0 World Cup Round of 16 win over Sweden.

items.title: Mexico oust Ecuador, 1st WC KO win in 40 years
items.summary: Mexico won their first World Cup knockout stage match in 40 years after dispatching Ecuador 2-0 in front an electric crowd at Estadio Azteca on Tuesday.

items.title: Mbappé sets record to lead France past Sweden
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé broke the record for most goals in World Cup knockout matches and moved within a single goal of Lionel Messi's overall tournament mark, as his two goals led France to a 3-0 win over Sweden.

items.title: England's Tuchel: Big-name WC exits can 'calm us'
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel has said surprise early World Cup exits for Germany and Netherlands will "calm our minds" and help England not to "over-expect" when facing Congo DR.

items.title: Pulisic says he is fit, ready to go for USMNT
items.summary: United States men's national team attacker Christian Pulisic said he is ready to go the distance in the team's World Cup round-of-32 match against Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday, no matter how long the game lasts.

items.title: FIFA clarifies crucial call to disallow Germany go...
items.summary: FIFA explained Tuesday the tighter regulations that resulted in Germany having a potentially game-winning goal disallowed in their shock elimination from the World Cup.

items.title: ⭐ Paris Hilton, Brad Pitt top celebs at World Cup
items.summary: Celebrities aren't hard to find at the 2026 FIFA World Cup in North America.
